ogTitle: Seven Mile Beach vs Shannon: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Seven Mile Beach and Shannon.
heading: Seven Mile Beach vs Shannon — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Seven Mile Beach, Tasmania and Shannon, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Seven Mile Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Shannon (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Seven Mile Beach or Shannon safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Seven Mile Beach has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Shannon scores 25/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Seven Mile Beach scores 19/100 for bushfire risk. Shannon scores 50/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Seven Mile Beach or Shannon?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Seven Mile Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
